label: Attio
description: Modern, flexible CRM with custom objects — connect Anton via an access token from your Attio developer settings.

form.title: Connect Attio
form.subtitle: Paste your Attio access token to give Anton read/write access to your workspace.

methods.label: Access Token
methods.description: Paste a personal access token from Attio's developer settings. Quickest path — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate an Attio Access Token

1. Open [Attio](https://app.attio.com) and log in to your workspace.
2. Click your workspace name or avatar in the bottom-left corner to open workspace settings.
3. Navigate to **Settings → Apps & Integrations → Developer**.
4. Click **Create token** (or **New token** / **+** depending on your plan).
5. Give the token a descriptive name like `Anton`.
6. Select the scopes Anton needs — at minimum **Read** access to records, lists, and notes; add **Write** if you want Anton to create or update records.
7. Click **Create** and copy the token immediately — it is shown only once.
8. Paste the token into the **Access Token** field below.

## Notes

- Tokens are workspace-scoped; the permissions you chose at creation time are fixed. Generate a new token to change scopes.
- If you lose the token, revoke it in the same **Developer** panel and create a fresh one.
- Each token is tied to the workspace you were viewing when you created it.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Personal access token from Attio Settings → Apps & Integrations → Developer. Shown only once at creation.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Authorize via Attio's OAuth flow using your own OAuth app credentials from the Attio developer portal. Better for multi-workspace deployments.
methods.how_to:
## Set Up Attio OAuth 2.0

1. Open [Attio](https://app.attio.com) and go to **Settings → Apps & Integrations → Developer**.
2. Click **Create OAuth App** and fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and a redirect URI of `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
3. After saving, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app detail page.
4. Paste both values into the fields below and click **Submit**.
5. A browser window will open to Attio's consent screen — log in and approve the requested scopes.
6. Anton stores the resulting refresh token locally.

## Notes

- OAuth apps are created per-workspace in the same **Developer** panel as personal tokens.
- If you need to revoke access, delete the OAuth app or revoke the token from the developer settings panel.
- Consult the [Attio OAuth docs](https://developers.attio.com/docs/oauth2) for the full list of available scopes.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your OAuth app in Attio Settings → Apps & Integrations → Developer.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID; copied from the same OAuth app detail page.
